fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Iowa Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity iowa, how to transfer occupational therapist license to iowa, occupational therapist compact iowa, iowa occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Iowa Board of Physical and Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing (DIAL)
board.mailing_address: Iowa Board of Physical and Occupational Therapy, Iowa Department of Health and Human Services, Bureau of Professional Licensure, Lucas State Office Building, 321 E. 12th Street, Des Moines, Iowa 50319-0075

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Iowa.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register for an Online Account
steps.description: Go to the Iowa HHS Online Services portal (ibplicense.iowa.gov) and click 'Register' to create a new user account. You will need to provide personal information and verify your email.

steps.title: Initiate Your Application
steps.description: Log in to your Iowa HHS Online Services account. Select 'Initial Application' and choose the 'Occupational Therapist (OT)' license type to begin the application for licensure by endorsement.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within the Iowa HHS Online Services portal, including personal details, educational history, and professional experience.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Iowa Board of Physical and Occupational Therapy. Submit verification of licensure from all states where you currently hold or have held a license. Complete any necessary criminal background check authorization as prompted by the Iowa HHS Online Services portal.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable $120 endorsement application fee electronically through the Iowa HHS Online Services portal.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Iowa Board of Physical and Occupational Therapy via the Iowa HHS Online Services portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Iowa OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Iowa has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Iowa?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Iowa
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Iowa, you must create an account and submit your application and all supporting documents through the Iowa HHS Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Iowa HHS Online Services portal and click 'Register' to create a new user account. Follow the prompts to enter your information, create a username and password, and verify your email address before logging in to start an application.
